comments.body: Operator-attested evidence for the CLAUDE.local.md criterion: edited /Users/joeoakhart/rebar/CLAUDE.local.md (local-only, git-ignored, not in any commit) — added to the top 'BEFORE STARTING ANY TICKET' worktree blockquote a bolded instruction to 'cd into the worktree and run every subsequent command — edits, gates, rebar, the ticket close, and git — from inside it', mirroring the CLAUDE.md change in this commit. Gerrit change: https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/620 (commit f335267cc).

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Agents get confused about which code the completion verifier reviews on close. The
close gate runs `verify_completion` with `ref="HEAD", fetch=False, repo_root=<the
checkout the command runs from>` (`src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:183`), so it
verifies the committed `HEAD` of **whichever checkout the `transition … closed` command is
run from** — NOT `origin/main`, and NOT necessarily the worktree where the edits were made.
The observed failure mode: an agent edits in a worktree but runs the close from the root of
the main checkout, so the verifier reads the wrong (unchanged) tree.

## What

Two coordinated changes (do not add meta-commentary framing them as "defense in depth" —
agents only need the actionable behavior):

1. **Document the behavior factually** — make the docs state that the close-gate verifier
   reviews the committed `HEAD` of the checkout the close command runs from, and that you
   must run close from the worktree/branch that holds the code to be verified.
2. **Fix the guidance** — repo rules and the two agent skills must instruct the agent to
   `cd` into the worktree it creates and run all subsequent commands (edits, gates, rebar,
   close, git) from inside it.

## Scope

- `docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md` — close-gate sentence + reconcile the "cwd/branch decoupling"
  statement so `ref=HEAD` (the CLI close path) reads as resolved relative to the invoking
  checkout.
- `docs/llm-framework.md` — the close-gate section.
- `CLAUDE.md` — completion-verifier/close-gate guidance; and the "work in a fresh worktree"
  guidance (add the `cd`-into-worktree instruction).
- `CLAUDE.local.md` (local-only; not committed) — worktree guidance.
- `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` and
  `examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md` — the worktree-creation step.

Out of scope (kept lean): reconciling ADR-0005's decoupling note is optional; MCP/CLI
docstring one-liners are secondary and would trip the reference-doc drift gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md` states the close gate verifies the committed `HEAD` of
      the checkout/worktree the `transition … closed` command runs from (not `origin/main`,
      not the main checkout), and directs running close from the worktree holding the code.
- [ ] `docs/repo-snapshot-gates.md`'s "cwd/branch decoupling" statement is reconciled so it
      no longer reads as contradicting the `ref=HEAD` close path (HEAD is resolved relative
      to the invoking checkout).
- [ ] `docs/llm-framework.md` close-gate section notes the verifier reads the committed
      `HEAD` of the checkout the close command runs from.
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` completion-verifier/close-gate guidance tells the agent to run close from
      the worktree/branch that contains the code to be verified.
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` "work in a fresh worktree" guidance instructs the agent to `cd` into the
      worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it.
- [ ] [operator-attested] `CLAUDE.local.md` (local-only, git-ignored — not in the committed
      tree, so not codebase-verifiable) worktree guidance instructs the agent to `cd` into the
      created worktree and run all subsequent commands from inside it, consistent with the
      `CLAUDE.md` instruction.
- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` Phase 1 instructs the agent to `cd`
      into the created worktree and run subsequent commands from there.
- [ ] `examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md` worktree step instructs the agent to
      `cd` into the created worktree and run subsequent commands from there.
- [ ] The B-layer edits (rules + skills) are plain operational instructions; they do NOT
      explain a "defense in depth" rationale.
- [ ] `make lint` passes on the change.

file_impact.reason: clarify close gate verifies HEAD of invoking checkout + reconcile cwd/branch decoupling
note close gate reads HEAD of the invoking checkout

compiled_state.title: Document that the close gate verifies HEAD of the invoking checkout, and make agents cd into their worktree
